prs.title: Feature/extraction
prs.body:
> 01_extraction: Joins raw F1 tables and filters for the Hybrid Era (2014-Present).

What: 
>Ingests raw F1 data and creates a unified master file.

Why: 
>Filters the data to the 2014+ era to ensure sponsorship analysis is based on modern performance.

prs.title: Refactor: Modular UI with user-friendly dashboard
prs.body:
## Changes

- **Modular codebase** : Split app.py into clean tab modules (prediction_dashboard, country_comparison, grid_advisor) + shared utils.py
- **Random Forest default** : Set as default model everywhere
- **User-friendly UI**  : Added plain-English KPI descriptions, simplified chart captions, removed technical jargon from Grid Advisor
- **HF deployment**  :  Verified working on Hugging Face Spaces

prs.title: Integrate Grid Advisor Agent Pipeline (Milestone 2)
prs.body:
**Summary of Changes:**
- **UI:** Added the new `Grid Advisor` tab with inputs mapped correctly.
- **Formatting:** Formatted the output to securely display Forecast Summaries at the top, and mapped the Risk Periods out strictly following your clean text layout with dynamic severity colors. 
- **Dependencies:** Updated `requirements.txt` to seamlessly include all necessary LangGraph and HuggingFace dependencies.
- **Import Fix:** Renamed the `Agent_Pipeline`'s internal model loader to `agent_model_loader.py` to elegantly resolve a Python execution overlap, strictly adhering to your rule of not touching the original `Demo_and_Hosting` version.

prs.title: feat: implement Composite Pattern and Dynamic Discounts UI
prs.body: This PR introduces the Composite Pattern for handling item bundles/combos and updates the UI to support dynamic discount strategies (Flat vs Percentage). It also integrates the Observer pattern for post-sale event handling.

prs.title: feat: implement Observer Pattern for post-sale event handling
prs.body: Implemented the Observer Pattern using Node.js event emitters to natively fan-out post-sale events. It delegates completely separate responsibilities like Audit Logging and mock SMS notifications out to loosely coupled observers, respecting the Single Responsibility Principle.

prs.title: feat: implement Strategy Pattern for discounts
prs.body: Refactored SaleService to delegate discount calculations to a dedicated Strategy Pattern. Added Flat, Percentage, and No-Discount strategies with a Factory for dynamic instantiation.

prs.title: refactor: enforce encapsulation in items controller
prs.body:
### What We Changed:
- Defined `getUnprintedItems` and `markMultipleAsPrinted` directly inside `ItemRepository.js` and properly encapsulated them with JSDoc comments.
- Modified `itemsController.js` to completely remove raw Prisma querying (where it was calling `_getPrisma()`) and instead strictly call the new Repository methods.

### System Design / OOP Principles Applied:
- **Encapsulation & Abstraction:** We successfully hid the underlying Database/ORM logic from the Controller. Controllers shouldn't be communicating with Prisma directly; that is the job of the Repository class.
- **Cleaner Architecture:** Adhering strictly to the **Repository Pattern** simplifies the controller down to just HTTP routing and honors the Single Responsibility Principle.

prs.title: Refactor tabs in app.py and remove pipeline section
Add Streamlit GUI to Demo_and_Hosting and update README
prs.body:
## What this PR does

Adds the interactive Streamlit-based GUI to the `Demo_and_Hosting/` folder and updates the main [README.md](cci:7://file:///Users/priyanshutomar/Desktop/genai_capstone_solar/gui/README.md:0:0-0:0) to reflect this addition.

## Key details

- Models are loaded directly from the existing [Models/solar_models.zip](cci:7://file:///Users/priyanshutomar/Desktop/genai_capstone_solar/EMHIRES-NASA-SolarPrediction/Models/solar_models.zip:0:0-0:0) archive — no files extracted, no changes to Models folder
- Supports both Linear Regression and Random Forest model selection
- All feature counts and labels match the actual trained model (34 features: 5 numeric + 29 country one-hot columns)
- No changes to any other folder (Pipeline, Training, Models, Datasets, etc.)

## How to run

```bash
cd Demo_and_Hosting
pip install -r requirements.txt
streamlit run app.py

prs.title: Add Streamlit GUI
prs.body:
Adds the interactive Streamlit web app (SolarIntel) to the Demo_and_Hosting folder.

What is included:
- app.py: Streamlit dashboard with four tabs (Prediction Dashboard, Pipeline Explorer, Country Comparison, Project Info)
- model_loader.py: Loads both Linear Regression and Random Forest models from Final_Pipeline
- requirements.txt: streamlit, pandas, numpy, plotly, scikit-learn
- README.md: Setup and usage instructions

Also adds solar_model_rfr.pkl to Final_Pipeline (extracted from Models/solar_models.zip) so both models are available without unzipping.

Updates the main README to describe the Demo_and_Hosting folder.

To run locally:
cd Demo_and_Hosting
pip install -r requirements.txt
streamlit run app.py

prs.title: feat: implement linear regression training and evaluation pipeline
prs.body:
##  Summary
Implemented the Training and Evaluation module for predicting solar **Capacity Factor** using a baseline Linear Regression model.

---

##  Implementation Details

- Implemented time-aware 80/20 train-test split to prevent temporal leakage  
- Trained baseline **Linear Regression** model for Capacity Factor prediction  
- Evaluated performance using:
  - Mean Absolute Error (MAE)
  - Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE)
  - R² Score  
- Computed coefficient-based feature importance  
- Ranked top 10 most impactful predictors influencing solar output  

---
##  Why Linear Regression?

Capacity Factor is a continuous variable (0–1 range), making this a regression problem.  
Linear Regression was selected as a baseline model due to:

---

prs.title: feat: One-Hot Encoding Pipeline for Country Variable
prs.body:
## Summary
Implemented one-hot encoding for the `Country` feature to prepare the dataset for ML model training.

## Changes Made
- Applied pandas get_dummies() with dtype=int
- Generated binary columns for each country
- Verified encoded feature columns
- Ensured compatibility with linear regression models

## Why
Country is a categorical variable and needs encoding before training ML models. One-hot encoding preserves interpretability and avoids ordinal bias.

prs.title: Add Deploy Preview Workflow for Vercel Integration
prs.body:
## Description
This PR adds a comprehensive GitHub Actions workflow for automated Vercel deployment previews, addressing the enhancement request in issue [#289](https://github.com/SASTxNST/Website_SAST/issues/289).

---

## Changes Made
- **Added Deploy Preview Workflow:** `.github/workflows/deploy-preview.yml` with full CI/CD pipeline.
- **Updated `.gitignore`:** Added `.vercel` directory to ignore Vercel-specific config files.

---

## Workflow Features
- ✅ **Automated Deployment:** Triggers on all pull requests (PRs)
- ✅ **Vercel Integration:** Uses official Vercel action for seamless deployments
- ✅ **Preview URLs:** Automatically posts live preview links for each PR
- ✅ **Build Validation:** Runs build steps and only deploys successful builds
- ✅ **Environment Isolation:** Each PR gets its own isolated deploy preview

---

## Testing & Validation
- 🧪 **Thoroughly tested in my fork:** ([r0c0y/Website_SAST](https://github.com/r0c0y/Website_SAST))
- 🧪 **Successfully merged PR:** [#1](https://github.com/r0c0y/Website_SAST/pull/1) in fork (green checks)
- 🧪 **Preview deployments working:** Valid, up-to-date preview URLs and automatic comments
- 🧪 **Build/install steps verified:** `npm install`, `npm run build` succeed without errors

---

## Setup Instructions for Maintainers

*To enable preview deployments for this workflow in the official repo:*

**1. Get Vercel Project & Org ID**
   - Link repo with Vercel CLI:
     ```
     npm install -g vercel
     vercel login
     vercel link
     ```
   - After linking, open `.vercel/project.json` and copy the `"projectId"` and `"orgId"` values.

**2. Generate a Vercel Token**
   - Visit: [https://vercel.com/account/tokens](https://vercel.com/account/tokens)
   - Click "Create" to generate a new token.
   - Set **scope** to "Full Account" and **expiration** to "Never".
   - Copy and save the token string (displayed once after creation).

**3. Add GitHub Repository Secrets**
   - Go to **Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions** in this repository.
   - Add the following secrets (case-sensitive!):
     - `VERCEL_TOKEN`: (from step 2)
     - `ORG_ID`: (from step 1)
     - `PROJECT_ID`: (from step 1)
     - `GITHUB_TOKEN`: (already available by default in GitHub Actions)

---

## Benefits
- 🚀 **Faster Review Process:** Reviewers see live previews for every PR
- 🔍 **UI/UX Validation:** Visual changes can be confirmed before merging
- 🛡️ **Risk Reduction:** Catches build/deploy issues before hitting main
- 📱 **Cross-device Testing:** Preview URLs open on any device
- 🔄 **Automatic Updates:** New PR commits automatically update the deploy preview

---

## References
- Fixes [#289](https://github.com/SASTxNST/Website_SAST/issues/289) — Enhancement: Add deploy preview workflow for better collaboration

> _Note: This contribution follows best open-source practices—tested locally, validated on my fork, and is now ready for upstream integration. If you need additional help setting up or testing these secrets, let me know!_

prs.title: feat: add deploy preview workflow for Vercel
prs.body:
This PR adds a GitHub Actions workflow for Vercel PR deploy previews, solving [issue #289](https://github.com/SASTxNST/Website_SAST/issues/289).

Explains: triggers on pull_request, builds with npm, posts preview link, secrets required: VERCEL_TOKEN, ORG_ID, PROJECT_ID.

Reference the steps you followed if you like.
